blocks.headline: Three lots. Six weeks. Then we move on.
blocks.subheadline: We carry three single-origins at a time — one washed African, one washed or honey Latin, one wet-hulled Indonesian. The rotation runs about six weeks. When something is gone, it's gone, and that's by design.

cards.title: Konga · Yirgacheffe
cards.description: Washed Ethiopia. Bergamot, jasmine, white peach. 250g, $22. Best as filter — the kind of cup you set down between sips.

cards.title: Finca La Esperanza
cards.description: Honey-process Colombia. Brown sugar, stone fruit, cocoa. 250g, $19. Our house espresso this month — pulls thick and sweet.

cards.title: Sumatra Lintong
cards.description: Wet-hulled Indonesia. Earthy, herbal, syrupy. 250g, $18. The right choice if you take it with milk and want the coffee to win.

blocks.text: The vocabulary, if you want it

We write tasting notes on butcher paper above the bar. They're not a test. They're a reading of what the cup is doing today, from the people who pulled it. If you taste something we didn't write down, you're not wrong — coffee is generous that way.

blocks.content:
## How we source

Three of our six current relationships are direct — meaning we know the producer's name, we've seen the farm in photos or in person, and we pay above the Fair Trade floor by a margin that actually matters to the producer's bottom line. The other three come through a small importer in Oakland we've worked with since the first year.

### What "direct trade" actually means here

It is not a certification. It is a claim about a relationship, and like any relationship it varies in depth. For us it means: we taste lots before we buy them, we pay in advance when a producer needs it, and we send back samples and notes after every roast so the next harvest is informed by what worked.

### Why we don't carry decaf year-round

Good decaf is hard to find and we'd rather not carry it than carry it half-heartedly. When we do — usually a Swiss Water process Colombian around the holidays — we'll tell you. Until then, we have a great pot of caffeine-free chamomile.

### A note on freshness

We roast on Tuesdays and ship on Wednesdays. If you order online, your beans arrive two to four days off the roast. They're at their best from day five to day three weeks. After a month, they're still fine, just less interesting. We don't sell anything older than six weeks.

blocks.title: Beans, every two weeks, on us choosing.
blocks.description: Subscribe and we'll pick the bag for you each rotation. Cancel anytime. The first bag ships free.
